former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919}} |神事 = Jingara-mochi（{{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Moro Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) Ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|First torii]] [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Second torii]] '''Moro Shrine'''（もろじんじゃ）is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}. Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県流山市三輪野山619', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} [[JA:茂侶神社 (流山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11617027}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Moro Shrine |画像 = [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) haiden.JPG|260px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = Jingara-mochi（{{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Moro Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) Ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|First torii]] [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Second torii]] '''Moro Shrine'''（もろじんじゃ）is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089|1=Katsushika district}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943|1=Matsudo}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800|1=1598}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505|1=Edo River}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804|1=Tōbu Urban Park Line}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846|1=Hatsuishi Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699|1=Tsukuba Express}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689|1=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570|1=Nagareyama-centralpark Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870|1=Nagareyama Line}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674|1=Nagareyama Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Chiba） * {{CRD|1000293193|I want to know about the "Jingara-mochi ritual" held every January at Moro Shrine in Miwano-yama, Nagareyama.|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagareyama Central Library|1=UNKNOWN}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もろしんしや なかれやまし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moro Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:流山市の建築物]] [[Category:流山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = Jingara-mochi（{{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Moro Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) Ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|First torii]] [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Second torii]] '''Moro Shrine'''（もろじんじゃ）is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089|1=Katsushika district}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943|1=Matsudo}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800|1=1598}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505|1=Edo River}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804|1=Tōbu Urban Park Line}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846|1=Hatsuishi Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699|1=Tsukuba Express}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689|1=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570|1=Nagareyama-centralpark Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870|1=Nagareyama Line}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674|1=Nagareyama Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Chiba） * {{CRD|1000293193|I want to know about the "Jingara-mochi ritual" held every January at Moro Shrine in Miwano-yama, Nagareyama.|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagareyama Central Library|1=UNKNOWN}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もろしんしや なかれやまし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moro Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:流山市の建築物]] [[Category:流山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11617027}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Moro Shrine |画像 = [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) haiden.JPG|260px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = Jingara-mochi（{{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Moro Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) Ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|First torii]] [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Second torii]] '''Moro Shrine'''（もろじんじゃ）is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089|1=Katsushika district}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943|1=Matsudo}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800|1=1598}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505|1=Edo River}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804|1=Tōbu Urban Park Line}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846|1=Hatsuishi Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699|1=Tsukuba Express}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689|1=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570|1=Nagareyama-centralpark Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870|1=Nagareyama Line}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674|1=Nagareyama Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Chiba） * {{CRD|1000293193|I want to know about the "Jingara-mochi ritual" held every January at Moro Shrine in Miwano-yama, Nagareyama.|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagareyama Central Library|1=UNKNOWN}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もろしんしや なかれやまし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moro Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:流山市の建築物]] [[Category:流山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = Jingara-mochi（{{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Moro Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) Ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|First torii]] [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Second torii]] '''Moro Shrine'''（もろじんじゃ）is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089|1=Katsushika district}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943|1=Matsudo}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800|1=1598}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505|1=Edo River}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804|1=Tōbu Urban Park Line}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846|1=Hatsuishi Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699|1=Tsukuba Express}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689|1=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570|1=Nagareyama-centralpark Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870|1=Nagareyama Line}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674|1=Nagareyama Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Chiba） * {{CRD|1000293193|I want to know about the "Jingara-mochi ritual" held every January at Moro Shrine in Miwano-yama, Nagareyama.|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagareyama Central Library|1=UNKNOWN}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moro Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:流山市の建築物]] [[Category:流山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11617027}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Moro Shrine |画像 = [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) haiden.JPG|260px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = Jingara-mochi（{{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Moro Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) Ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|First torii]] [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Second torii]] '''Moro Shrine'''（もろじんじゃ）is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089|1=Katsushika district}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943|1=Matsudo}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800|1=1598}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505|1=Edo River}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804|1=Tōbu Urban Park Line}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846|1=Hatsuishi Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699|1=Tsukuba Express}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689|1=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570|1=Nagareyama-centralpark Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870|1=Nagareyama Line}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674|1=Nagareyama Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Chiba） * {{CRD|1000293193|I want to know about the "Jingara-mochi ritual" held every January at Moro Shrine in Miwano-yama, Nagareyama.|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagareyama Central Library|1=UNKNOWN}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moro Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:流山市の建築物]] [[Category:流山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11617027}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Moro Shrine |画像 = [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) haiden.JPG|260px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = Jingara-mochi（{{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Moro Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) Ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|First torii]] [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Second torii]] '''Moro Shrine'''（もろじんじゃ）is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089|1=Katsushika district}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943|1=Matsudo}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800|1=1598}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505|1=Edo River}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804|1=Tōbu Urban Park Line}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846|1=Hatsuishi Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699|1=Tsukuba Express}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689|1=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570|1=Nagareyama-centralpark Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870|1=Nagareyama Line}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674|1=Nagareyama Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Chiba） * {{CRD|1000293193|I want to know about the "Jingara-mochi ritual" held every January at Moro Shrine in Miwano-yama, Nagareyama.|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagareyama Central Library|1=UNKNOWN}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moro Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:流山市の建築物]] [[Category:流山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = Jingara-mochi（{{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Moro Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) Ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|First torii]] [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Second torii]] '''Moro Shrine'''（もろじんじゃ）is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089|1=Katsushika district}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943|1=Matsudo}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800|1=1598}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505|1=Edo River}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804|1=Tōbu Urban Park Line}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846|1=Hatsuishi Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699|1=Tsukuba Express}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689|1=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570|1=Nagareyama-centralpark Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870|1=Nagareyama Line}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674|1=Nagareyama Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Chiba） * {{CRD|1000293193|I want to know about the "Jingara-mochi ritual" held every January at Moro Shrine in Miwano-yama, Nagareyama.|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagareyama Central Library|1=UNKNOWN}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moro Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:流山市の建築物]] [[Category:流山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = Jingara-mochi（{{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Moro Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) Ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|First torii]] [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Second torii]] '''Moro Shrine'''（もろじんじゃ）is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089|1=Katsushika district}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943|1=Matsudo}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800|1=1598}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505|1=Edo River}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804|1=Tōbu Urban Park Line}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846|1=Hatsuishi Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699|1=Tsukuba Express}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689|1=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570|1=Nagareyama-centralpark Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870|1=Nagareyama Line}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674|1=Nagareyama Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Chiba） * {{CRD|1000293193|I want to know about the "Jingara-mochi ritual" held every January at Moro Shrine in Miwano-yama, Nagareyama.|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagareyama Central Library|1=UNKNOWN}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moro Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagareyama, Chiba]] [[Category:流山市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Miwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = Jingara-mochi（{{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Moro Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) Ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|First torii]] [[File:Moro-jinja (Nagareyama) ninotorii.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Second torii]] '''Moro Shrine'''（もろじんじゃ）is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089|1=Katsushika district}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943|1=Matsudo}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800|1=1598}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505|1=Edo River}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804|1=Tōbu Urban Park Line}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846|1=Hatsuishi Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699|1=Tsukuba Express}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689|1=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570|1=Nagareyama-centralpark Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870|1=Nagareyama Line}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674|1=Nagareyama Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Chiba） * {{CRD|1000293193|I want to know about the "Jingara-mochi ritual" held every January at Moro Shrine in Miwano-yama, Nagareyama.|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagareyama Central Library|1=UNKNOWN}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moro Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagareyama, Chiba]] [[Category:流山市の歴史]]

Its former name was "Miwa Shrine." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}（おおものぬしのみこと） === History === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsushika District|Q11621089|WD=Q11621089|1=Katsushika district}} Moro Shrine" as a small shrine, and is regarded as one candidate shrine. There are two other candidate shrines (in {{ill|lt=Matsudo|Q320943|WD=Q320943|1=Matsudo}} and {{ill|lt=Funabashi|Q271417|WD=Q271417|1=Funabashi}})（see "{{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}}"）. The first appearance of Moro Shrine itself in the national chronicles is in the entry for 12 November, Jōgan 13 (817), in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}: "Moro-no-kami was granted {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Senior Fifth Rank, Upper." Later, on 25 September, Gangyō 3（{{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}）, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank, Upper. The founding of the shrine is unknown. Its deity Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto is considered a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, and the shrine name "Moro" is thought to come from "Moro" in the old name of Mt. Miwa, "Mimoro-yama."There is also a theory that it derives from "mori," meaning a sacred grove where gods dwell. For this reason, the plateau where the shrine stands is also called "Mt. Miwa"（{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}）. From remains of a jingū-ji, old torii remains on the former western approach at Hirai, and place-name origins such as Yagi-gō, it is inferred that the shrine once had an extensive {{ill|lt=sacred precinct|Q11588853|WD=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. In {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 3（{{ill|lt=1598|Q6800|WD=Q6800|1=1598}}）, excavation of the {{ill|lt=Edo River|Q1194505|WD=Q1194505|1=Edo River}} divided the sacred area. This shrine lies on the east bank. As a result, during the {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} era a branch shrine was established on the west bank, and {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Yoshikawa)|Q119929491|WD=Q119929491|lt=Miwa Shrine|1=Miwa Shrine}} was founded. Also, the shrine received an annual offering of 25 koku from the {{ill|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662|WD=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}. === Precincts === * Honden * Haiden - the roof tiles were re-laid in Heisei 7. Adjacent to the precincts are remains of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Ritual === * Jingara-mochi : At the Obisha (New Year rite) on {{ill|lt=January 8|Q2234|WD=Q2234|1=January 8}}, kagami-mochi is offered before the deity and then parishioners tear it apart. The way it breaks was used to divine the year's harvest. It is designated by {{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}} as an intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Nagareyama-designated intangible folk cultural property === * Jingara-mochi ritual === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagareyama|Q328625|WD=Q328625|1=Nagareyama}}{{ill|lt=Miwano-yama|Q11357427|WD=Q11357427|1=Miwano-yama}}619 ; Access * {{ill|lt=Tōbu Noda Line|Q76804|WD=Q76804|1=Tōbu Urban Park Line}} {{ill|lt=Hatsuishi Station|Q5681846|WD=Q5681846|1=Hatsuishi Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Tsukuba Express|Q1377699|WD=Q1377699|1=Tsukuba Express}} / Tōbu Noda Line {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station|Q841689|WD=Q841689|1=Nagareyama-ōtakanomori Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * Tsukuba Express {{ill|lt=Nagareyama-centralpark Station|Q850570|WD=Q850570|1=Nagareyama-centralpark Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） * {{ill|lt=Ryūtetsu Nagareyama Line|Q1055870|WD=Q1055870|1=Nagareyama Line}} {{ill|lt=Nagareyama Station|Q11556674|WD=Q11556674|1=Nagareyama Station}}（about 25 minutes on foot） === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Moro Shrine|Q11617025|WD=Q11617025|1=Moro Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Moro-jinja (Nagareyama)}} * {{Archive.today |url= http://www.kanko.chuo.chiba.jp/kanko/1035/ |title= Moro Shrine（Chiba Prefecture website, "Comprehensive guide to sightseeing in Chiba"） |date=20121219004811}} * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/8836/ Moro Shrine]（Chiba official tourism information site - Marugoto e! Chiba） * {{CRD|1000293193|I want to know about the "Jingara-mochi ritual" held every January at Moro Shrine in Miwano-yama, Nagareyama.|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagareyama Central Library|1=UNKNOWN}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Moro Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagareyama, Chiba]] [[Category:流山市の歴史]]
